expectations.evidence: "Primary workflow: workflows/triage-issue.md -- the 'Triage Issue' workflow for bug reports with duplicate detection."

expectations.text: Extracts error signature, component (payment), and symptom (checkout failing)
expectations.evidence: Table shows: Error signature = "Connection timeout", Component = "Payment service", User-facing symptom = "Checkout fails".

expectations.text: Runs at least 2 different search queries with different JQL strategies
expectations.evidence: Runs 6 different search strategies: by error message text, by component, by summary keywords, broader keyword combinations (2 queries), time-scoped recent bugs, plus a Confluence CQL search.

expectations.text: Searches for both open and resolved issues
expectations.evidence: Strategy 1 searches all bugs (no status filter). Strategy 2 filters to open (statusCategory != Done). Strategy 5 searches recent bugs regardless of status. Analysis includes checking resolved issues as "Possible regression".
Presentation format shows assessment labels: "LIKELY DUPLICATE", "POSSIBLE REGRESSION", "LIKELY RELATED". Each match rated as exact duplicate, likely related, possible regression, or unrelated.

expectations.text: Offers options: create new, comment on existing, or show more details
expectations.evidence: Section 5 presents: "Would you like me to: (a) Add a comment to DO-XXX... (b) Create a new bug ticket and link it... (c) Create a brand new standalone bug ticket?" Plus Option D for regression reopening.

expectations.text: Asks which project if not specified, or searches broadly
expectations.evidence: The approach assumes project DO without asking. No evidence of asking about which project or searching broadly across projects.
"Summary follows references/bug-report-templates.md format" and "Description follows the 'Standard Bug Template' structure from references/bug-report-templates.md."
